name: Readable
description: Readable — Automated code documentation tool that generates comments and docstrings from code analysis.

seo.title: Readable — Code Assistants / Agent Tooling
seo.meta_description: Readable: Readable — Automated code documentation tool that generates comments and docstrings from code analysis.

when_to_use: Analyzing content readability and grade level via API
Improving text clarity for target reading audiences automatically
Building content quality workflows with readability scoring
Checking documentation complexity before publishing for developers

pros: Automates docstring and comment generation at scale
Useful for backfilling docs on legacy codebases
Supports common languages with IDE plugins
cons: Generated comments often state the obvious
Can produce misleading docs if code intent is unclear
Copilot and Cursor cover this for free
Standalone tools are being squeezed by general assistants
review.verdict: A narrow tool that general-purpose AI assistants already do well enough, hard to justify separately.
review.best_for: Legacy projects needing bulk docstring generation in one pass
review.not_for: Teams already using Copilot or Cursor for inline documentation

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Freemium with free tier; no peak-lock evidence
